title: Work Experience Reflection (RA)
description: Reflect on working relationships, conflict resolution, regulations, and reporting procedures.

questions.prompt: Describe the organisational procedures to deal with and report any problems that can affect working relationships.
questions.exampleContext: You consistently disagree with a colleague on how a process should be run, causing delays. Why do organizations have formal procedures to help resolve such conflicts?

questions.prompt: Describe the difficulties that can occur in working relationships and how you can resolve them.
questions.exampleContext: Miscommunication can easily lead to frustration. What are some common difficulties that arise when people fail to communicate effectively in a team?

questions.prompt: Describe the sorts of attitudes and requests that are likely to create conflict or negative responses.
questions.exampleContext: Imagine a team member who constantly dismisses other people's ideas aggressively. How can certain attitudes quickly escalate into workplace conflict?

questions.prompt: Describe how The Working Time Regulations affect how you should be treated at work.
questions.exampleContext: Fatigue can lead to dangerous mistakes in an engineering environment. How do The Working Time Regulations protect you from being overworked?

questions.prompt: Describe how The Equality Act affects how you should be treated at work.
questions.exampleContext: A diverse workforce brings different perspectives to problem-solving. How does The Equality Act ensure that everyone is treated fairly regardless of their background?

questions.prompt: Describe the importance of making a contribution to improving working practices and procedures.
questions.exampleContext: You notice a way to machine a part 10% faster. Why is it important for employees to proactively suggest improvements to working practices?

questions.prompt: Explain when you should act on your own initiative and when you should ask for help from others.
questions.exampleContext: You notice a safe, simple way to organize your tools better, but you also notice a complex machine making a strange noise. When is it appropriate to act on your own initiative versus asking for help?

questions.prompt: In the workplace generally, who would you need to report to in the event of a problem?
questions.exampleContext: You have encountered a problem that you do not have the authority or knowledge to fix. Who is the correct person to report this to in a typical workshop hierarchy?
